heading: Policy research, data collection, analysis and modeling, evaluation, and dissemination
label: 9108 Policy research, data collection, analysis and modeling, evaluation, and dissemination
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (b)(3), was in the original “this Act” and was translated as reading “this title”, meaning title II of , known as the Museum and Library Services Act, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 94–462

Amendments2018—, substituted “Policy research, data collection, analysis and modeling, evaluation, and dissemination” for “Policy research, analysis, data collection, and dissemination” in section catchline.Pub. L. 115–410, § 5(1)Subsecs. (a) to (f). , added subsecs. (a) to (f) and struck out former subsecs. (a) to (e) which required the Director annually to conduct policy research, analysis, and data collection to improve the Nation’s museum, library, and information services, set out requirements and objectives for the research, required dissemination of the results, and provided contract authority. Former subsec. (f) redesignated (g).Pub. L. 115–410, § 5(3)Subsec. (g). , redesignated subsec. (f) as (g).Pub. L. 115–410, § 5(2)Subsec. (g)(1). , substituted “each of the fiscal years 2020 through 2025” for “fiscal year 2011 and such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2012 through 2016”.Pub. L. 115–410, § 5(4)2010— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “From amounts described in sections 9123(c) and 9176(b) of this title, the Director shall carry out and publish analyses of the impact of museum and library services. Such analyses—Pub. L. 111–340“(1) shall be conducted in ongoing consultation with—“(A) State library administrative agencies;“(B) State, regional, and national library and museum organizations; and“(C) other relevant agencies and organizations;“(2) shall identify national needs for, and trends of, museum and library services provided with funds made available under subchapters II and III of this chapter;“(3) shall report on the impact and effectiveness of programs conducted with funds made available by the Institute in addressing such needs; and“(4) shall identify, and disseminate information on, the best practices of such programs to the agencies and entities described in paragraph (1).”

L. 111–340“(1) shall be conducted in ongoing consultation with—“(A) State library administrative agencies;“(B) State, regional, and national library and museum organizations; and“(C) other relevant agencies and organizations;“(2) shall identify national needs for, and trends of, museum and library services provided with funds made available under subchapters II and III of this chapter;“(3) shall report on the impact and effectiveness of programs conducted with funds made available by the Institute in addressing such needs; and“(4) shall identify, and disseminate information on, the best practices of such programs to the agencies and entities described in paragraph (1).”

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Director shall regularly support and conduct, as appropriate, policy research, data collection, analysis and modeling, evaluation, and dissemination of information to extend and improve the Nation’s museum, library, and information services.

To enhance and expand the capacity of museums, libraries, and information services to anticipate, respond to, and meet the evolving needs of communities and the public, including by identifying trends and developments that may impact the need for and delivery of services.

To provide information and data on the role, value, and impact of museum, library, and information resources, including the identification of trends and potential gaps in the availability and use of museum and library services by their communities and the public.
To measure the effectiveness of museums, libraries, and information services throughout the United States, including the impact of Federal programs authorized under this chapter.
To identify indicators and outcomes that can be used to create enhancements to the efficiency and efficacy of museum, library, and information services.
To promote advancement and growth in museum, library, and information services through sharing of best practices and effective strategies in order to better serve the people of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: museum, library, and information services at the national, State, local, and regional levels; and

children.label: B
children.text: international communications and cooperative networks.

To support and enhance collaborative professional networks and consortia that use shared, meaningful, and actionable data analysis and modeling to advance museum, library, and information services and address community needs.

children.heading: Authority to contract and enter into other arrangements
children.label: c Authority to contract and enter into other arrangements
children.text: The Director is authorized to enter into grants, contracts, cooperative agreements, and other arrangements with Federal agencies, public and private organizations, and other entities with expertise the Director determines appropriate, to further the objectives described in subsection (b) and to carry out the responsibilities under subsection (f).

children.label: A
children.text: State library administrative agencies; and

children.label: B
children.text: National, State, tribal, and regional museum and library organizations; and

children.label: A
children.text: cooperative networks of geographic- or discipline-based museums and libraries; and

children.label: B
children.text: other applicable agencies, organizations (including international organizations), entities (including entities with expertise in the fields of data collection, analysis and modeling, and evaluation), and community stakeholders.

children.heading: Assistance to museums and libraries
children.label: e Assistance to museums and libraries
children.text: The Director shall provide technical support and assistance (and other resources, to the extent practicable) to ensure consistency in data reporting and help the museum and library fields with meeting the objectives of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the results, data, reports, findings, studies, surveys, and other information obtained under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the means and approaches by which the objectives described in subsection (b) were accomplished; and

children.label: C
children.text: information regarding the manner and extent to which collaboration and consultation were conducted, as required by subsection (d).

children.heading: Formats to be used
children.label: 2 Formats to be used
children.text: The information described in paragraph (1) shall be shared in formats that facilitate access and ease of use and are searchable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $3,500,000 for each of the fiscal years 2020 through 2025.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: 2 Availability of funds
children.text: Sums appropriated under paragraph (1) for any fiscal year shall remain available for obligation until expended.
